title: cerastium glomeratum var. corollinum
intro: Cerastium glomeratum var. corollinum, also known as Sticky Mouse-ear, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family. It is native to Europe and Asia and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial and expectorant properties. Cerastium glomeratum var. corollinum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote healing and alleviate symptoms.
